title: I Married Marge

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfectly integrated character moment, maximum economy

jokes.text: Barnacle Bill's Home Pregnancy Test
jokes.setup: Marge questions the brand choice for pregnancy test
jokes.punchline: Homer chose it because it came with a free corncob pipe
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer logic - choosing medical products based on free novelty items

jokes.text: Pirate-themed pregnancy test instructions
jokes.punchline: Instructions written in pirate speak: 'Ahoy, mateys. If the water turns blue, a baby for you'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant commitment to the pirate pregnancy test bit - absurd but well-executed

jokes.text: Test turns pink instead of blue or purple
jokes.setup: Test should turn blue (pregnant) or purple (not pregnant)
jokes.punchline: It turns pink, confusing everyone
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation then subverts with third option

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated D'oh!
jokes.explanation: Second D'oh of episode - good character consistency

jokes.text: If a test should fail... to a doctor set sail
jokes.setup: Continuing to read pirate pregnancy test instructions
jokes.punchline: Even the failure instructions are in pirate speak
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to the bit - extends the pirate theme logically

jokes.text: Homer's pregnancy symptoms
jokes.punchline: Homer has 'tightness in chest and profuse sweating' - his anxiety symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Homer's sympathetic pregnancy symptoms are actually anxiety

jokes.text: Your mother just broke her leg
jokes.setup: Kids ask why mom is going to doctor
jokes.punchline: Homer's obvious lie about broken leg
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer bad lying - perfectly in character

jokes.text: I smell a bun in the oven
jokes.setup: Homer's failed attempt to hide pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately sees through it with pregnancy idiom
jokes.explanation: Smart kid sees through dad's deception - good use of idiom

jokes.text: You're a machine, Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart congratulates Homer on his virility
jokes.explanation: Appropriately inappropriate comment from Bart about his dad

jokes.text: For me, it's diaper changes and midnight feedings. Doesn't Mom do that? Yeah, but I hear about it.
jokes.punchline: His biggest burden is hearing about Marge doing the work
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer characterization - his selfishness revealed in perfect comic structure

jokes.text: I threw up more than your mother
jokes.setup: Bart asking if Homer was like this during previous pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Homer had worse morning sickness than Marge
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Homer's sympathetic pregnancy symptoms theme

jokes.text: Those were idealistic days-- The candidacy of John Anderson, the rise of Supertramp
jokes.punchline: Calling obscure politician and mediocre band 'idealistic'
jokes.explanation: Good cultural satire - treating minor figures as major historical moments

jokes.text: Homer, you turn the blades too fast. The golfers are complaining.
jokes.setup: Homer working at mini golf course
jokes.punchline: His job is manually turning windmill blades and he's too enthusiastic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd job detail - Homer can't even handle manual windmill operation

jokes.text: Keep this up and someday you'll be the guy who hands out the putters
jokes.punchline: The 'promotion' is an equally menial job
jokes.explanation: Good satire of meaningless workplace hierarchies and false promises

jokes.text: I thought it was over. You had a problem turning blades. You overcame it. The feel-good story of the year.
jokes.punchline: Bart reduces it to a trivial feel-good story formula
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on storytelling clichés, perfectly delivered by Bart

jokes.text: It's not our fault our generation has short attention spans. We watch an appalling amount of TV.
jokes.setup: Kids not paying attention to Homer's story
jokes.punchline: Lisa blames their TV consumption while being self-aware about it
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary delivered through Lisa - self-aware criticism of TV culture

jokes.text: Don't you ever, ever talk that way about television
jokes.punchline: Homer's passionate defense of television
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - defending TV with religious fervor

jokes.text: Homer's mid-sentence forgetting
jokes.setup: Homer getting angry and starting to lecture
jokes.punchline: He completely loses his train of thought mid-rant
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer brain malfunction - builds to nothing perfectly

jokes.text: Okay, girls, you can smoke now
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement that smoking break has begun
jokes.explanation: Good period detail joke - captures 80s casual smoking culture

jokes.text: I don't know what you see in that ugly meatball
jokes.punchline: Calling Homer an 'ugly meatball'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty/Selma insult - crude but specific imagery

jokes.text: If you like being pawed by something fat and lazy, we could get a cat. It would leave less hair on the couch.
jokes.punchline: Comparing him unfavorably to a cat, saying cat would leave less hair
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - starts with cat comparison then makes it worse with hair detail

jokes.text: Marge, get your butt out here!
jokes.setup: Marge just defended Homer as 'sensitive and sweet'
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately yells crudely for her
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - immediately contradicts Marge's defense of Homer

jokes.text: Do you mean like will apes be our masters?
jokes.setup: Marge asks if he thinks about the future
jokes.punchline: Homer's mind goes to Planet of the Apes scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - turns practical question into sci-fi absurdity

jokes.text: What do you think about? Well, girls. I mean boys! I mean you.
jokes.setup: Marge asks what he thinks about at work
jokes.punchline: Homer accidentally reveals he thinks about other women, tries to cover
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's wandering mind and bad recovery

jokes.text: Well, thank you, Mr. Blow-The-Picture-For-Me
jokes.setup: Homer spoils Empire Strikes Back ending
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic response to having movie spoiled
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration with movie spoilers, well-phrased sarcasm

jokes.text: You're as pretty as Princess Leia and as smart as Yoda
jokes.punchline: His romantic comparison uses Star Wars characters
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization - romantic but filtered through pop culture

jokes.text: Actually, she was singing about God. Oh, well, he's always happy. No, wait, he's always mad.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused theology about God's mood
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer confusion about religious concepts

jokes.text: Someday I'll buy you a real castle. You don't have to do that. Whoo, good.
jokes.punchline: Relieved when Marge says he doesn't have to follow through
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - makes grand gesture then immediately grateful to avoid it

jokes.text: It must be the Champale talking
jokes.punchline: Homer attributes it to the cheap malt liquor
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecation and class humor - cheap beer reference

jokes.text: This castle is impregnable
jokes.setup: At mini golf castle, about to have sex
jokes.punchline: Uses 'impregnable' when they're about to conceive Bart
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning - castle can't be taken, but Marge is about to get pregnant

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Third D'oh of episode - appropriate reaction to pregnancy news

jokes.text: So You've Ruined Your Life pamphlet
jokes.punchline: Pamphlet title suggests pregnancy ruins young men's lives
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny medical pamphlet - subverts expected supportive literature

jokes.text: You'll never do any better. You lucky bum. The fish jumped right in the boat.
jokes.punchline: Tells Homer Marge is out of his league with fishing metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa wisdom - insults Homer while giving practical advice

jokes.text: All you got to do is whack her with the oar
jokes.punchline: Advice becomes accidentally violent-sounding
jokes.explanation: Extends the fishing metaphor in unexpectedly dark direction

jokes.text: Damn it, where's that card?
jokes.punchline: He needs cue cards for his proposal
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence - can't remember proposal without notes

jokes.text: In your face, everybody! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrates by taunting imaginary rivals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - turns romantic moment into competitive celebration

jokes.text: The baby's name will be Kool Moe Dee Simpson
jokes.punchline: Homer wants to name baby after 80s rapper
jokes.explanation: Good 80s period detail - absurd baby name from pop culture

jokes.text: All the kids will call him Larry Fairy... Screwy Louie... Slob... Puke... Mucus
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why certain names won't work
jokes.punchline: Increasingly crude rhyming insults for each name
jokes.explanation: Good escalating wordplay - increasingly gross rhymes

jokes.text: Bart, cart, dart, e-art-- Nope. Can't see any problem with that.
jokes.setup: Homer testing name 'Bart' for rhyming problems
jokes.punchline: Misses obvious 'fart' rhyme, invents nonsense 'e-art'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - overthinks to avoid obvious answer, creates nonsense word

jokes.text: Get this punched every time. The tenth wedding is on the house.
jokes.punchline: Wedding chapel has frequent customer punch card system
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cynical - treats sacred ceremony like fast food loyalty program

jokes.text: No matter how many times I hear that, it always makes me laugh
jokes.setup: Homer says marriage will last forever
jokes.punchline: Jaded officiant finds this claim hilarious
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy - wedding official's cynicism about marriage durability

jokes.text: You may not look like Ted Bessell, but you're just as nice
jokes.setup: Marge talking about Homer as ideal husband
jokes.punchline: Compares him to obscure 70s TV actor, emphasizes he's not attractive
jokes.explanation: Gentle insult disguised as compliment - obscure reference adds authenticity

jokes.text: By the power vested in me by the state gaming commission
jokes.punchline: Official's authority comes from gambling regulators, not religious body
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail - wedding authority from gambling rather than church

jokes.text: Hey, love birds, keep it down
jokes.punchline: Complaint from neighboring room about noise
jokes.explanation: Predictable honeymoon noise complaint - functional but uninspired

jokes.text: Baby to Marge. Baby to Marge. Wah! Wah! Over.
jokes.punchline: Uses it like walkie-talkie to simulate crying baby
jokes.explanation: Good Homer childishness - treats baby monitor as toy communication device

jokes.text: I heard radiation can make you sterile. Now you tell me.
jokes.setup: Homer applying to nuclear plant for better pay
jokes.punchline: Homer's mock disappointment about sterility coming too late
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about unwanted pregnancy and radiation exposure

jokes.text: Smithers, you keg-meister, you
jokes.punchline: Evil billionaire using college party slang
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Burns using dated fraternity terminology

jokes.text: It takes me a long time to learn anything. I'm kind of a goof-off-- That will do.
jokes.setup: Job interview asking about worst qualities
jokes.punchline: Homer gives brutally honest self-assessment, gets cut off
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty sabotaging job interview - completely in character

jokes.text: We're all going to die! Run!
jokes.punchline: Homer panics and flees instead of following procedure
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer panic response - completely wrong but perfectly in character

jokes.text: Yeah. The doctor.
jokes.setup: Homer promises baby will see a man with good job first
jokes.punchline: Marge points out doctor will deliver the baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Homer's promise - economical and smart

jokes.text: What a crappy candle. You've ruined our vacation.
jokes.punchline: Tourist's extreme overreaction to poor candle quality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - minor flaw ruins entire vacation

jokes.text: Handle first, handle first
jokes.punchline: Marge's panicked instruction about knife safety
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - implies Homer handed knife blade-first

jokes.text: I thought you said you liked dogs
jokes.punchline: Implies he was attacked by dogs at work
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - reveals unexpected job hazard

jokes.text: Our model is the trapezoid that guarantees each investor an 800% return
jokes.punchline: Claims it's not pyramid scheme, it's trapezoid (basically same shape)
jokes.explanation: Clever scam logic - geometric technicality to avoid pyramid scheme label

jokes.text: Uh-oh! The cops!
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic and presumably fleeing when police arrive
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - confident criminal immediately becomes coward

jokes.text: We break thumbs
jokes.punchline: Casual threat of physical violence for debt collection
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical threat - two words convey serious menace

jokes.text: Repossessing stuff is the hardest part of my job
jokes.punchline: Gentle repo man claims emotional difficulty with job
jokes.explanation: Humanizes typically villainous character - unexpectedly sympathetic repo man

jokes.text: Shut up with that pen scratching down there
jokes.punchline: Sisters interrupt emotional moment with petty complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect sisters characterization - ruining emotional moment with trivial annoyance

jokes.text: Can we have a can of frosting for lunch?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be responsible household head
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately ask for terrible meal choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - undermines Homer's responsibility claim immediately

jokes.text: This taco is full of hair
jokes.punchline: Disgusting food quality complaint
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor - functional but relies on disgust rather than cleverness

jokes.text: They get doughnuts! All the colors of the rainbow.
jokes.setup: Homer envious of nuclear plant workers
jokes.punchline: He's most impressed by colorful doughnuts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - fascinated by simple workplace perks

jokes.text: You want fries with that?
jokes.setup: Marge wants her husband by her side
jokes.punchline: Homer responds with fast food worker script
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer can't break out of work mode even for emotional reunion

jokes.text: Holy cow, you're as big as a house!
jokes.punchline: Homer's tactless comment about her size
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer insensitivity - ruins romantic moment with blunt observation

jokes.text: They won't even tell me what's in the secret sauce
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he can't leave job
jokes.punchline: His career ambition is learning fast food sauce recipe
jokes.explanation: Good Homer priorities - treats mundane information as valuable career knowledge

jokes.text: The oil is burning my finger
jokes.setup: Homer gives Marge makeshift onion ring engagement ring
jokes.punchline: The hot oil is actually hurting her
jokes.explanation: Good romantic gesture gone wrong - practical problems with food jewelry

jokes.text: I knocked you up
jokes.setup: Marge asks why she married him
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude, reductive answer
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer bluntness - reduces romantic relationship to crude facts

jokes.text: 'I knocked you up.' 'No, because I love you.'
jokes.setup: Marge asking why she married Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude vs. Marge's romantic response
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing their different perspectives

jokes.text: The hunter has become the hunted
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration about role reversal
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns dramatics - treats mundane situation with theatrical intensity

jokes.text: Treat me like dirt-- I'll still kiss your butt and call it ice cream!
jokes.punchline: Extreme self-deprecating offer with gross metaphor
jokes.explanation: Memorably disgusting metaphor for Homer's desperate employment pitch

jokes.text: Feisty, yet spineless
jokes.punchline: Perfect contradictory description of Homer's personality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character assessment - captures Homer's contradictory nature perfectly

jokes.text: This man failed the aptitude test and got trapped in a closet on his way out
jokes.punchline: Lists Homer's failures as if they're achievements
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - failing so badly it becomes impressive to Burns

jokes.text: Only in America could I get a job!
jokes.setup: Homer gets hired despite complete incompetence
jokes.punchline: Celebrates American dream while being obviously unqualified
jokes.explanation: Smart satire - celebrates American opportunity while highlighting its absurdity

jokes.text: Simpson, eh? I'll remember that name.
jokes.punchline: Sets up their future antagonistic relationship
jokes.explanation: Good setup for future episodes - Burns' positive first impression of Homer

jokes.text: Don't ever call me that
jokes.punchline: Harsh rejection of any family connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect sisters hostility - rejects even accidental family bonding

jokes.text: This is my wife, and my kid. I'm paying for this delivery.
jokes.punchline: Homer asserts authority through payment
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - stands up for family using economic logic

jokes.text: Tomorrow, I'm a nuclear technician! Good God!
jokes.punchline: Doctor's horrified reaction to Homer in nuclear job
jokes.explanation: Good reaction joke - doctor realizes the terrifying implications

jokes.text: As long as he's got eight fingers and eight toes, he's fine by me
jokes.punchline: Homer's low standards for baby health
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting humor - Homer's minimal expectations for his child

jokes.text: He did that on purpose! How could he? He's only ten minutes old.
jokes.setup: Baby Bart apparently does something annoying
jokes.punchline: Homer attributes malicious intent to newborn, Marge points out impossibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Homer-Bart antagonistic relationship - starts immediately

jokes.text: Homer! I'm not pregnant! Yeah! Whoa! Excellent, Marge! Yes!
jokes.punchline: Excessive celebration of negative pregnancy test
jokes.explanation: Good ending - family's overwhelming relief creates comedy through excess

tmdb_overview: A trip down memory lane to the early days of Simpson wedded bliss, when Marge was with child and Homer was without a job.
insight: Eighty jokes in 17 minutes sustains a brisk pace despite middling impact scores.
